source_pr.title: executor: revert #25730 to fix #28011
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #28011

Problem Summary: as described in https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/28011 , kv pairs converted from locks is visible to union-scan, which leads to duplicated rows (one from snapshot and the other from mem-index-reader).

### What is changed and how it works?

Since https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/13694 has introduced a better solution to the problem of accumulated locks, thus we can simply revert #25730 .

[Here](https://clinic.pingcap.com.cn/portal/#/orgs/33/clusters/7213372357103198046?from=1679493001&to=1679495159) is the benchmark result. It shows that there is a slight regression in point-get workload.

![2023-03-22_223208](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6850317/226940812-982673cc-b271-4b78-a1a5-3adcfc19c372.png)
![2023-03-22_223259](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6850317/226940843-a7147ad4-4427-4ae4-94b7-e09ecdf4b4cc.png)


### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```
